WRONG.question: In what year was the first transatlantic cable successfully operated?
WRONG.model_response:
The first transatlantic cable was successfully operated in **1858**. The first official message was sent by Queen Victoria to U.S. President James Buchanan. However, the cable failed after only three weeks due to insulation breakdown and voltage issues. Despite its short lifespan, it marked the first successful transatlantic telegraph communication.
